title: MSE-4

{{Listen|filename=Mousey.flac|title=MSE-4s squealing|description=Five MSE-4s squeal as they flee from Darth Vader}}
The '''MSE-4''' was a wheeled [[Labor droid/Legends|labor droid]] that was made by [[Rebaxan Columni/Legends|Rebaxan Columni]] and used by the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] at its [[Magma/Legends|lava]] [[Mining/Legends|mines]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]]. A model of the [[MSE-series|MSE-Series]], the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] had been launched by the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], when the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] [[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|terminated]] the [[Separatist Council/Legends|Separatist leadership]] taking refuge at a [[Klegger Corp Mining Facility/Legends|mining facility]] on the planet. A group of MSE-4s scuttered ahead of Vader as he approached his prey, jumping over a gap in the flooring to flee the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith's]] presence.

==Characteristics==
The MSE-4 was a model of [[Labor droid/Legends|labor droid]] manufactured by [[Rebaxan Columni/Legends|Rebaxan Columni]] as part of their [[MSE-series|MSE-Series]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] line. Owned by members of the [[Chadra-Fan/Legends|Chadra-Fan]] [[Species/Legends|species]], the company invented the MSE droid for use on<ref name="NEGD" /> [[Chad/Legends|Chad]],<ref name="GG4">''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]''</ref> the Chadra-Fan [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. A local pet of the [[Planet/Legends|world]] named the [[pleeky]] formed the basis of the MSE's design, which Rebaxan Columni hoped the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] market would find cute. Also known as mouse droids, MSEs' usage was only limited by their owners' imagination.<ref name="NEGD" />

A [[Class five droid/Legends|fifth-degree]] droid,<ref name="NEGD" /> the MSE-4 was less than 0.3 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height<ref name="Size" /> and, similar to the [[MSE-6 series repair droid/Legends|MSE-6]],<ref name="NEGD" /> had a [[Color/Legends|black]] box-shaped body with four wheels underneath for mobility. Capable of jumping over gaps in floorings, the mouse droid made squealing noises<ref name="ROTS" /> and was deployed by operators of [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar's]] [[Magma/Legends|lava]] [[Mining/Legends|mines]] to [[Scout/Legends|scout]] for [[Life support/Legends|life-support]] leaks.<ref name="NEGD" />

==History==
Test runs of the MSE-4 and [[MSE-5 General Purpose Droid|MSE-5]] models were launched by Rebaxan Columni before the marketing efforts for and mass-production of the MSE-6, which resulted in the company's [[Currency/Legends|bankruptcy]] toward the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]<ref name="NEGD" /> between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist Alliance]].<ref name="ROTS" /> In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> [[Separatist Council/Legends|Separatist leaders]] were accompanied by MSE-4s while sheltering at their<ref name="ROTS" /> [[Klegger Corp Mining Facility/Legends|mining facility headquarters]] on Mustafar, a hazardous mining planet ruled by the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]].<ref name="CL2005">[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']]</ref>

To end the Clone Wars, [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] sent his new [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], to [[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|eliminate]] the Separatist leaders on Mustafar. As Vader walked through a hallway of the Separatist headquarters, five squealing MSE-4s scrambled from the approaching [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]], with each mouse droid jumping over a break in the flooring and dispersing in a disorderly fashion into the facility's [[Separatist Council war room/Legends|control room]] that housed Vader's quarry. Soon after, Vader sealed the room's exits and proceeded to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the Separatist inhabitants.<ref name="ROTS" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|What I want is a miniature box droid, like the one Chewbacca scares [in Episode IV]. I want these little quail running down the hallway ahead of Anakin. The idea is 'rats leaving a sinking ship.'|George Lucas speaks to Industrial Light & Magic's Rob Coleman and Roger Guyett|<ref name="Making of ROTS">''[[The Making of Star Wars Revenge of the Sith]]''</ref>}}
[[File:MSE-6_btm.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The MSE-4 was based on the mouse droid model featured in ''A New Hope'' (pictured).]]
The MSE-4 appears in the [[2005]] [[prequel trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']],<ref name="ROTS" /> and was identified in the [[2006]] reference title ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'', written by [[Daniel Wallace]].<ref name="NEGD" /> The scene featuring the MSE-4 was shot on [[August 25]], [[2003]], at Stage 2 of [[Fox Studios, Sydney]]. For the scene, director [[George Lucas]] asked [[Industrial Light & Magic]] (ILM) on [[November 18]], [[2004]] to [[Wikipedia:Rendering (computer graphics)|render]] a model of the mouse droid seen in the [[1977]] [[original trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. Lucas' intention was to use the quailing droid to imitate rats leaving a sinking ship.<ref name="Making of ROTS" /> Later, Lucas requested ILM's [[Rob Coleman]], [[Roger Guyett]], and [[John Knoll]] make the single [[Wikipedia:Computer-generated imagery|computer-generated]] mouse droid they had rendered a quarter of its size,<ref name="60 Minutes">{{YouTube|video=Mhd47kudxts|text=George Lucas interview on 60 Minutes about Star Wars Episode III (2005)|channel=channel/UCYRuqoSXqrID_VA7qDj6kUQ|name=Andreas F.}}</ref> resulting in the creation of the smaller MSE-4 droids.<ref name="ROTS" />
